heading: Secretary of the Air Force: successors to duties
label: 9017 Secretary of the Air Force: successors to duties
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)8017(a)8017(b)5:626–1(b).5:626–1(c).(less (a)), .Sept. 19, 1951, ch. 407, § 10265 Stat. 327In subsection (a), the word “person” is substituted for the words “officer of the United States”. The words “until a successor is appointed” are omitted as surplusage.Subsection (b) is substituted for 5:626–1(c) and states the effect of .section 8544(b) of this title

References in Text, referred to in text, was repealed and a new section 3347 was enacted by , , , and, as so enacted, no longer contains provisions authorizing the President to direct temporary successors to duties. See , Government Organization and Employees.Section 3347 of title 5Pub. L. 105–277, div. C, title I, § 151(b)112 Stat. 2681–611section 3345 of Title 5Oct. 21, 1998

Amendments2021—Par. (5). added par. (5).Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 8017 of this title1994—Pars. (3), (4). added par. (3) and redesignated former par. (3) as (4).Pub. L. 103–3371986— struck out subsec. (a) designation, substituted in par. (2) “, in the order prescribed by the Secretary of the Air Force and approved by the Secretary of Defense” for “in order of their length of service as such”, and struck out subsec. (b) which read as follows: “Performance of the duties of the Secretary by the Chief of Staff or any officer of the Air Force designated under shall not be considered as the holding of a civil office within the meaning of .”Pub. L. 99–433section 3347 of title 5section 973(b) of this title1968—Subsec. (b). substituted “” for “”.Pub. L. 90–235section 973(b) of this titlesection 8544(b) of this title1966— substituted “” for “” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 89–718section 3347 of title 5section 6 of title 5

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.text: The Under Secretary of the Air Force.
The Assistant Secretaries of the Air Force, in the order prescribed by the Secretary of the Air Force and approved by the Secretary of Defense.
The General Counsel of the Department of the Air Force.
